pairs.question: Which electoral division in New South Wales is named after Woollarawarre Bennelong?
pairs.answer: The Division of Bennelong is the electoral division in New South Wales named after Woollarawarre Bennelong, an Aboriginal man who was befriended by Arthur Phillip, the first Governor of New South Wales.

pairs.question: Who has been the representative for the Division of Bennelong since the 2022 federal election?
pairs.answer: Jerome Laxale of the Labor Party has been the representative for the Division of Bennelong since the 2022 federal election.

pairs.question: What are the local government areas included in the Division of Bennelong?
pairs.answer: The Division of Bennelong includes all of the local government areas of Ryde, Lane Cove, and Hunter's Hill, as well as parts of Willoughby and Parramatta.

pairs.question: Which former Australian Prime Minister represented the Division of Bennelong from 1974 to 2007?
pairs.answer: John Howard, who served as the Prime Minister of Australia from 1996 until 2007, represented the Division of Bennelong from 1974 to 2007.

pairs.question: What significant event occurred in the Division of Bennelong during the 2007 federal election?
pairs.answer: In the 2007 federal election, Labor candidate Maxine McKew won the Division of Bennelong on a thin 1.4-point margin, becoming the first Labor MP to represent the seat.

pairs.question: Why was the Division of Bennelong vacant from 11 November 2017 to 16 December 2017?
pairs.answer: The Division of Bennelong was vacant from 11 November 2017 when Liberal candidate John Alexander resigned due to the 2017–18 Australian parliamentary eligibility crisis after confirming he was a dual citizen and therefore ineligible to sit in parliament.

pairs.question: How did demographic changes and electoral boundaries impact the Division of Bennelong's political status?
pairs.answer: Modern-day electoral boundaries and demographic changes have contributed to the Division of Bennelong becoming an increasingly marginal seat, shifting from historically being a fairly safe Liberal seat to a more competitive electoral environment.
